insights:
DeepSeek V4 Flash is the single best deal in the market today. With an intelligence score of 40 out of 60 and a blended price of just $0.0600 per million tokens, it achieves a value score of 666.7—the highest by a large margin. It delivers 103 tokens per second and supports a full 1,000K context window. No other model comes close to this combination of capability and cost efficiency, making it the clear first choice for developers who want maximum intelligence per dollar.

Two notable runners-up offer different trade-offs. MiMo-V2.5 also costs $0.0600 per million tokens but scores slightly lower in intelligence at 37 out of 60, giving it a value score of 616.7. Its reasoning and coding ability (arena code Elo 1430) can make it a viable alternative if that specific skill matters. For teams that need higher absolute intelligence, DeepSeek V4 Pro scores 44 out of 60 at $0.1800 per million tokens—triple the price but with a value score of 244.4, still reasonable. Its speed is lower at 68 t/s, and context remains 1,000K. Meanwhile, MiniMax M3 offers a similar intelligence of 44 at $0.2200 per million tokens, with faster speed (114 t/s) and the highest coding Elo (1495), but at a higher per-token cost.

For the most common cost-sensitive API use case, the recommendation is straightforward: use DeepSeek V4 Flash as your default. It covers the vast majority of production needs with strong intelligence, fast throughput, and a huge context window—all at the lowest price tier. If you occasionally need more reasoning power, DeepSeek V4 Pro offers a modest step up without an extreme price jump. Avoid models with lower value scores unless you have a very specific requirement like ultra-high speed (Step 3.7 Flash at 389 t/s) or maximum
